quiz.title: Unit 8 Quiz 10, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Mole Fractions, Molarity from Percent, and Gas Law Applications
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Mole Fraction Calculations, Molarity from Percent by Mass and Density, Gas Volume at STP, Atomic Mass from Gas Data

questions.question: A mixture contains 3 g of $\text{N}_2$ and $\text{O}_2$ with mole fraction of $\text{N}_2 = 0.6$. What is the mole fraction of $\text{O}_2$?
questions.explanation:
The sum of mole fractions in a mixture equals 1.

Mole fraction of O₂ = 1 - mole fraction of N₂ = 1 - 0.6 = 0.4

questions.question: What is the molarity of a 25% (by mass) NaCl solution if its density is 1.2 g/mL?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: 25% by mass means 25 g NaCl in 100 g solution.

Step 2: Volume of 100 g solution = mass/density = 100/1.2 = 83.33 mL = 0.08333 L

Step 3: Molar mass NaCl = 58.44 g/mol
Moles NaCl = 25/58.44 = 0.4278 mol

Step 4: Molarity = 0.4278/0.08333 = 5.13 M

questions.question: 112 mL of a diatomic gas weighs 0.5 g at 273 K and 1 atm. Find the mass of one atom of this gas.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: At STP, 22.4 L = 1 mol
Moles = 0.112 L / 22.4 L/mol = 0.005 mol

Step 2: Molar mass = mass/moles = 0.5/0.005 = 100 g/mol

Step 3: Diatomic gas → atomic mass = 100/2 = 50 g/mol

Step 4: Mass of one atom = 50 / $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $8.30 \times 10^{-23}$ g

questions.question: A gas mixture has 4 g of CO and CO₂, with mole fraction of CO = 0.4. Find the mole fraction of CO₂.
questions.explanation:
The sum of mole fractions in a mixture equals 1.

Mole fraction of CO₂ = 1 - mole fraction of CO = 1 - 0.4 = 0.6

questions.question: Calculate the molarity of a 10% (by mass) glucose solution if density is 1.05 g/mL.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: 10% by mass means 10 g glucose in 100 g solution.

Step 2: Volume of 100 g solution = 100/1.05 = 95.24 mL = 0.09524 L

Step 3: Molar mass glucose = 180.16 g/mol
Moles glucose = 10/180.16 = 0.0555 mol

Step 4: Molarity = 0.0555/0.09524 = 0.583 M

questions.question: 448 mL of a triatomic gas weighs 2 g at 273 K and 1 atm. Determine the molar mass of the gas.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: At STP, 22.4 L = 1 mol
Moles = 0.448 L / 22.4 L/mol = 0.02 mol

Step 2: Molar mass = mass/moles = 2/0.02 = 100 g/mol

questions.question: A mixture contains 5 g of H₂ and N₂ gases, with mole fraction of H₂ = 0.3. Find the mole fraction of N₂.
questions.explanation:
The sum of mole fractions in a mixture equals 1.

Mole fraction of N₂ = 1 - mole fraction of H₂ = 1 - 0.3 = 0.7

questions.question: What is the molarity of a 30% (by mass) HCl solution if density is 1.15 g/mL?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: 30% by mass means 30 g HCl in 100 g solution.

Step 2: Volume of 100 g solution = 100/1.15 = 86.96 mL = 0.08696 L

Step 3: Molar mass HCl = 36.46 g/mol
Moles HCl = 30/36.46 = 0.8228 mol

Step 4: Molarity = 0.8228/0.08696 = 9.46 M

questions.question: 336 mL of a diatomic gas weighs 1 g at 273 K and 1 atm. Find the mass of one atom of the gas.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: At STP, 22.4 L = 1 mol
Moles = 0.336 L / 22.4 L/mol = 0.015 mol

Step 2: Molar mass = 1/0.015 = 66.67 g/mol

Step 3: Diatomic gas → atomic mass = 66.67/2 = 33.33 g/mol

Step 4: Mass of one atom = 33.33 / $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $5.54 \times 10^{-23}$ g

questions.question: A mixture of O₂ and CO₂ contains 6 g total with mole fraction of O₂ = 0.25. Find mole fraction of CO₂.
questions.explanation:
The sum of mole fractions in a mixture equals 1.

Mole fraction of CO₂ = 1 - mole fraction of O₂ = 1 - 0.25 = 0.75

questions.question: Calculate the molarity of a 40% (by mass) NaOH solution if density is 1.5 g/mL.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: 40% by mass means 40 g NaOH in 100 g solution.

Step 2: Volume of 100 g solution = 100/1.5 = 66.67 mL = 0.06667 L

Step 3: Molar mass NaOH = 40.00 g/mol
Moles NaOH = 40/40.00 = 1.0 mol

Step 4: Molarity = 1.0/0.06667 = 15.0 M

questions.question: 560 mL of a tetraatomic gas weighs 3 g at 273 K and 1 atm. Find molar mass of the gas.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: At STP, 22.4 L = 1 mol
Moles = 0.560 L / 22.4 L/mol = 0.025 mol

Step 2: Molar mass = mass/moles = 3/0.025 = 120 g/mol

questions.question: A mixture has 2 g of He and Ne with mole fraction of He = 0.7. Find mole fraction of Ne.
questions.explanation:
The sum of mole fractions in a mixture equals 1.

Mole fraction of Ne = 1 - mole fraction of He = 1 - 0.7 = 0.3

questions.question: Determine the molarity of 20% (by mass) urea solution if density is 1.12 g/mL.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: 20% by mass means 20 g urea in 100 g solution.

Step 2: Volume of 100 g solution = 100/1.12 = 89.29 mL = 0.08929 L

Step 3: Molar mass urea = 60.06 g/mol
Moles urea = 20/60.06 = 0.333 mol

Step 4: Molarity = 0.333/0.08929 = 3.73 M

questions.question: 224 mL of a diatomic gas weighs 0.8 g at 273 K and 1 atm. Find mass of one atom of the gas.
questions.explanation:
Step 1: At STP, 22.4 L = 1 mol
Moles = 0.224 L / 22.4 L/mol = 0.01 mol

Step 2: Molar mass = 0.8/0.01 = 80 g/mol

Step 3: Diatomic gas → atomic mass = 80/2 = 40 g/mol

Step 4: Mass of one atom = 40 / $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $6.64 \times 10^{-23}$ g

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered mole fractions, molarity from percent, and gas calculations! 🧪📊
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the relationships between mass percent, density, and molarity.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on using the molar volume at STP (22.4 L/mol) correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Please review mole fraction concepts, molarity calculations, and gas law fundamentals.
